results.rationale: cxt is allocated in _rl_nsearch_init (line 219) and returned to noninc_search. On the normal path the resource is freed via _rl_nsearch_cleanup at line 434 (and via _rl_nsearch_abort at 419). However the early return at line 428 (if r < 0 return 1) leaves the loop without calling cleanup or abort, leaking cxt. The line 409 callback return is an intentional ownership transfer to the global _rl_nscxt, but the 428 path has no such transfer.
results.fix_suggestion: At line 428, call _rl_nsearch_cleanup/_rl_nsearch_abort (or xfree cxt) before returning so cxt is released on the r<0 path.

results.rationale: trap_command is allocated via savestring at line 964 but never freed on the return path at line 996. parse_and_execute does not take ownership of the string in bash; it parses a copy. Other trap-running functions in bash free this buffer, so the allocation leaks here on each invocation of the EXIT trap branch.
results.fix_suggestion: free(trap_command) before the return at line 996 (after parse_and_execute and the array_dispose call).
